description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 AI-platform-ops cluster: AI feature governance/risk posture vs one-change process audit vs SDLC lifecycle vs technical threat modeling, and against the shipped agent-governance-audit and ai-sdlc-operating-model.

cases.prompt: Assess the risk tier and governance readiness of our new customer-facing AI advisor before launch — oversight, accountability, disclosure, and documentation.
cases.reason: Risk-tiering and governance posture of an AI feature is this skill; agent-governance-audit checks one change's process compliance and ai-sdlc-operating-model defines the lifecycle, neither tiers feature risk.

cases.prompt: Did this PR follow our process? Check classification, approvals at each boundary, who merged, and whether the closeout evidence holds up.
cases.reason: Per-control PASS/FAIL audit of a specific change from PR evidence is agent-governance-audit; this skill reviews the feature's standing governance/risk posture.

cases.prompt: Define how humans and agents move work through our SDLC stages with entry/exit gates and per-stage authority.
cases.reason: The end-to-end human+agent lifecycle contract is ai-sdlc-operating-model; this skill reviews a feature's risk/governance, not the SDLC stage model.

cases.prompt: Enumerate the attack surface of this AI feature across the OWASP LLM Top 10 — injection, disclosure, tool misuse.
cases.reason: Technical threat enumeration is ai-threat-modeler; this skill covers organizational governance/risk (tier, oversight, accountability, obligations), not attack vectors.
